Amended Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One And To Req. For Production Of Documents, Set One, And To Produce Documents, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-21-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Farallon Associates Insurance Brokers' motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and request for production set #1. The motion is moot as responses have been served. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of the responses. The parties are ordered to meet and confer. The Court finds that plaintiff failed to respond in a timely fashion. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $405 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Off Calendar Jul-21-2006 Off Calendar

Second Amended Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers And Or Further Answers To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, And Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-21-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Farallon Associates Insurance Brokers' motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories (employment law) and special interrogatories set #1.
The motion is granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to serve further responses within 21 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that plaintiff made evasive responses and asserted unmeritorious objections. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $547.50 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Amended Ntc Of Mtn To Deem Req.'S For Admissions, Set One, Admitted, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-21-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Farallon Associates Insurance Brokers' motion to deem certain matters admitted.
The motion is moot as responses have been served. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of the responses. The parties are ordered to meet and confer. The Court finds that plaintiff failed to respond in a timely fashion. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $365 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
